name: Domaine du Tornet
meta_title: Domaine du Tornet — La Balme-de-Sillingy, Haute-Savoie
meta_description: 7-hectare lake, pumptrack, playground, Asian cherry trees, motorhome area. Free access year-round at La Balme-de-Sillingy, Haute-Savoie.

hero.lead: Seven hectares of lake, a pumptrack, a new playground, a fitness area, a jetty, and the national collection of Asian flowering cherry trees. The living heart of La Balme-de-Sillingy.

hero_alt: Flowering cherry tree on the shore of the Domaine du Tornet lake, La Balme-de-Sillingy

body.what_is:
The Domaine du Tornet — also known as the Domaine de loisirs du Tornet or Lac de La Balme — is a public leisure and sports area on the shore of a 7-hectare lake at the heart of La Balme-de-Sillingy. It is owned by the commune, which has been developing it since 2021. Entry is free, year-round.

It is not a campsite, an amusement park or a paid tourist facility. It is a genuine stretch of managed Savoyard countryside: free-access sports, a lake locals walk and push prams around, a new pumptrack, a playground opened in 2025, a jetty worth sitting on, and — a seasonal spectacle — a national collection of Asian flowering cherry trees that blazes pink between late March and mid-April.

activities.title: Lap of the lake
activities.description: Loop of approximately 2 km, no elevation gain, 30 minutes on foot. Compacted path, suitable for pushchairs and wheelchairs.

activities.title: Fishing
activities.description: Private lake managed by Balme Pêche Loisirs. Permit required, sold at the commune's tourist information point.

activities.title: Pumptrack
activities.description: 280 m, 3 routes marked by coloured lines. Suitable for mountain bike, BMX, skateboard, roller skates, balance bike and scooter.

activities.title: Street workout & fitness
activities.description: Strength training, gymnastics and freestyle area facing the lake. Separate fitness circuit for adults.

activities.title: Children's playground
activities.description: Large playground opened summer 2025, plus a dedicated toddler area.

activities.title: Pétanque
activities.description: Pétanque area available free of charge on site.

activities.title: Asian flowering cherry trees — Parc des Jardins
activities.description: The Domaine hosts the national collection of Asian flowering cherry trees (Haute-Savoie Parc des Jardins). Peak bloom: late March to mid-April.

activities.title: Electric bike hire
activities.description: Electric bikes available for hire on site. Connects directly to the La Belle Via greenway towards Annecy and Frangy.

activities.title: Motorhome area
activities.description: Dedicated motorhome area with 22 pitches (23rd under construction), direct walking access to the lake and restaurant.

practical_info.v: Free — redesigned late 2025 with EV charging points and coach parking
Lake circuit and main paths suitable for pushchairs and wheelchairs. Adapted equipment in the 2025 playground. Disabled parking spaces available.

how_to_get_there.car: From Annecy, take the A41 motorway towards Geneva, exit La Balme-de-Sillingy. At the main roundabout, follow the direction of Frangy — the Domaine is on the right.
how_to_get_there.public_transport: LIHSA line 61 connects Annecy to La Balme-de-Sillingy. The stop is a few minutes' walk from the Domaine. Timetables at sibra.fr.
how_to_get_there.bike: The La Belle Via greenway connects Annecy to Frangy via Sillingy and La Balme. The Domaine is directly on the route.

body.when_to_visit: The Domaine is open year-round. Peak moments: late March to mid-April for the cherry blossom; May–June for green spring conditions; July–August for peak family season (arrive early for calm); September–October for autumn light and low crowds; winter for a brisk walk.
body.events: The Domaine hosts the Fête du Lac, a vintage car rally, a family festival and a young farmers' event. Check the commune calendar for dates.

faq.q: Is the Domaine du Tornet free to visit?
faq.a: Yes. Access to the Domaine du Tornet is free and open year-round. Only fishing in the lake requires a permit, sold by Balme Pêche Loisirs.

faq.q: Can I bring my dog to the Domaine du Tornet?
faq.a: Yes. Dogs are welcome at the Domaine du Tornet on a lead. The lakeside circuit is particularly well suited for a walk with a dog.

faq.q: Is there parking at the Domaine du Tornet?
faq.a: Yes, a large free car park redesigned in late 2025, with EV charging points and a coach bay.

faq.q: Is the Domaine du Tornet pumptrack suitable for beginners?
faq.a: Yes. Opened in January 2023, the Domaine du Tornet pumptrack has 3 routes of increasing difficulty (marked by coloured lines), suitable for mountain bike, BMX, skateboard, roller skates, balance bike or scooter.

faq.q: When do the cherry trees bloom at the Domaine du Tornet?
faq.a: The national collection of Asian flowering cherry trees at the Domaine du Tornet blooms between late March and mid-April, depending on the weather. It is the most photographed moment at the Parc des Jardins.

faq.q: Can I stay overnight at the Domaine du Tornet by motorhome?
faq.a: Yes. The Domaine du Tornet has a dedicated motorhome area with 22 pitches (a 23rd is being added), with direct walking access to the lake and restaurant.

faq.q: Is there a restaurant at the Domaine du Tornet?
faq.a: Yes. The Chalet du Tornet is located directly on the Domaine, on the lakeside. Savoyard cuisine, bar open all day, terrace in season.

faq.q: Is the Domaine du Tornet accessible for people with reduced mobility?
faq.a: The lakeside circuit at the Domaine du Tornet is suitable for pushchairs and wheelchairs (compacted path, no significant gradient). The 2025 playground includes adapted equipment. Disabled parking spaces are available.

partners.name: Chalet du Tornet
partners.description: The only restaurant directly on the Domaine. Savoyard cuisine, Maison Baud charcuterie, lakeside terrace. Bar open all day. Open year-round.
